title: sidalcea asprella subsp. asprella
intro: Sidalcea asprella subsp. asprella, also known as Asprella, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the mallow family, Malvaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Sidalcea asprella subsp. asprella can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, but more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
